title: Schedule Keystone admin password rotation via operator Cr...
description:
# GitHub Issue #383: Keystone admin password rotation — operator-scheduled CronJob (Model B, Part 2 of #381)

**Blocked on / follow-up to:** #381 (Part 1, Model A — re-bootstrap on admin Secret change). This issue is **Part 2 (Model B)** of that work and was explicitly carved out as a separate change by #381's concrete boundary 10 and its first Non-Goal. It cannot land before #381 because Model B's entire value depends on Part 1's `ESO → Secret → operator → bootstrap Job` cutover path being in place — Model B only adds the *upstream* half (operator-scheduled generation of a new password and its delivery into OpenBao).

**Description:**

Part 1 (#381) closes the *application* half of admin-credential rotation: when the `keystone-admin` Secret's `password` byte changes, the controller re-runs the idempotent `keystone-manage bootstrap` Job and Keystone begins accepting the new password. What Part 1 deliberately does **not** do is *produce* a new password — it assumes some external actor (an operator running `bao kv put kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin password=<new>`, or a future scheduler) writes the new value into OpenBao, from where ESO's existing `keystone-admin` ExternalSecret (`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml`, `refreshInterval: 1h`, `creationPolicy: Owner`) syncs it into the cluster Secret.

Model B is that scheduler. It adds an **opt-in, feature-gated, operator-managed CronJob** that periodically generates a fresh strong admin password and delivers it into OpenBao via a `PushSecret`, so rotation happens on a schedule instead of depending on a human remembering to run `bao kv put`. The new password then reaches Keystone via *exactly* the Part 1 path — OpenBao → ESO → `keystone-admin` Secret → re-bootstrap — which is precisely why Model B can only land cleanly on top of Part 1.

This mirrors the split-compute-write rotation architecture already shipped for Fernet keys (`reconcile_fernet.go`) and credential keys (`reconcile_credential.go`) under CC-0081, with one structural difference: the production source of truth for the admin password is **OpenBao**, not an in-cluster Secret. So the apply side of the pipeline terminates in a `PushSecret` to `bootstrap/keystone-admin` rather than in an in-cluster production Secret update.

Concrete boundaries between what exists and what this issue adds:

1. **The Fernet/credential CronJob pattern is the template, and it is fully built.** `reconcileFernetKeys` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:45-151`) and `reconcileCredentialKeys` (`reconcile_credential.go:52-159`) already implement the complete shape Model B copies: (a) an operator-owned staging Secret the CronJob PATCHes into (`ensureStagingSecret`, `rotation_staging.go:121-147`), (b) a split RBAC Role — read-only on the production Secret, `get`+`patch` on the staging Secret only (`ensureFernetRotationRBAC`, `reconcile_fernet.go:159-217`), (c) a CronJob whose script computes the new material and PATCHes it onto the staging Secret with a `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` commit annotation (`fernetRotationCronJob`, `reconcile_fernet.go:286-421`), (d) an operator-side apply step that validates the staging output and commits it (`applyRotationOutput`, `rotation_staging.go:182-269`), and (e) a `PushSecret` backing the result up to OpenBao (`fernetKeysPushSecret`, `reconcile_fernet.go:435-461`, ensured via `secrets.EnsurePushSecret` at `reconcile_fernet.go:137`). Model B reuses all five primitives; only the leaf details (what the script generates, where the apply step writes) differ.

2. **A new feature-gate field on `BootstrapSpec` is required.** `BootstrapSpec` (`operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:388-407`) currently holds only `AdminUser`, `AdminPasswordSecretRef`, `Region`, `PublicEndpoint`. Model B adds an optional `PasswordRotation *PasswordRotationSpec` field (`spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation`), **default disabled**, mirroring the leaf-default convention of `FernetSpec` (`keystone_types.go:332-342`), `CredentialKeysSpec` (`:344-354`), and `TrustFlushSpec` (`:356-380`). Proposed fields: `enabled bool` (default `false`), `schedule string` (cron, e.g. default `"0 0 1 * *"` monthly), and `suspend bool` (pause the CronJob without deleting it, exactly as `TrustFlushSpec.Suspend` does). An optional `passwordLength int32` with a sane minimum and default may be added if generation length needs to be tunable. Because this is a real CRD schema change (unlike Part 1, which added zero spec fields), `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`, the controller-gen CRD bases under `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/`, and the Helm chart copy under `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/` **must** be regenerated and kept in lockstep (see the `check-crd-drift` skill).

3. **Defaulting and validation webhooks need to learn the new field.** Following the `TrustFlushSpec` precedent (`keystone_types.go:356-380` notes the defaulting webhook materializes the parent struct so leaf defaults fire deterministically), the defaulting webhook should materialize a default `schedule` when `passwordRotation.enabled` is true and `schedule` is empty, and the validating webhook should reject a malformed cron expression. The validating webhook is also the right place to enforce any Part-1 prerequisite invariants (e.g. that `adminPasswordSecretRef` resolves to the ESO-Owned `keystone-admin` Secret, since pushing a new password is pointless if ESO is not the source of truth for that Secret).

4. **The apply side terminates in OpenBao, not an in-cluster Secret — this is the one real divergence from the Fernet/credential template.** For Fernet/credential, `applyRotationOutput` (`rotation_staging.go:182-269`) commits the staging payload onto the *in-cluster production keys Secret*, and a separate `PushSecret` backs that Secret up to OpenBao. For the admin password, the production source of truth is OpenBao itself (ESO `creationPolicy: Owner` makes the in-cluster `keystone-admin` Secret a read-only mirror — see #381's "Migrating away from Owner" Non-Goal). Therefore the apply step must commit the new password to a **dedicated push-source Secret** (e.g. `<keystone>-admin-password-next`) that a `PushSecret` syncs to `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin`. The push-source Secret MUST be distinct from the ESO-Owned `keystone-admin` Secret, otherwise the `PushSecret` writer and ESO's `creationPolicy: Owner` reconciler fight over the same object every refresh. Once the new password lands in OpenBao, the existing `keystone-admin` ExternalSecret pulls it back into the `keystone-admin` Secret and Part 1 takes over.

5. **A new OpenBao write policy for the bootstrap admin path is required, and it is the most security-sensitive piece.** Today `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*` is **read-only**: `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl` grants only `read`, and the existing write policy `deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl` is *deliberately* scoped to the `…/keystone/+/fernet-keys` and `…/credential-keys` leaves and explicitly does **not** cover `bootstrap/*`. Model B needs a new, narrowly-scoped policy granting `create`/`update`/`read`/`delete` on **both** `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin` **and** `kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin` (ESO's KV-v2 provider writes the metadata path too — see the rationale block in `push-keystone-keys.hcl`), bound alongside on the same auth role that runs the admin-password `PushSecret`. `eso-management.hcl` must stay read-only so the CC-0083 audit invariant ("a leaked management-cluster ESO token on `eso-management` alone cannot write to OpenBao") is preserved. Granting write to the *admin bootstrap path specifically* is a higher-trust grant than the key-backup paths and should be called out in review.

6. **A new status sub-condition and its metrics label binding are required.** Fernet/credential set `FernetKeysReady`/`CredentialKeysReady`; Model B should add a `PasswordRotationReady` condition wired into the aggregate `subConditionTypes` list and into the `subReconcilerConditionTypes` metrics-label map so the Prometheus `condition_type` label resolves instead of showing `UNKNOWN` (see the `check-condition-coverage` skill — this is a hard gate). When `passwordRotation.enabled` is false, the sub-reconciler should be a clean no-op (and tear down any previously-created CronJob/staging Secret/RBAC/PushSecret) so disabling the feature returns to the Part-1-only steady state.

7. **The generation script follows the extracted-script convention (CC-0073).** Both rotation CronJobs embed their script from `scripts/` via `//go:embed` (`reconcile_fernet.go:40` → `scripts/fernet_rotate.sh`; `reconcile_credential.go:40` → `scripts/credential_rotate.sh`) so the shell is independently lintable and testable, and materialize it through an immutable ConfigMap (`config.CreateImmutableConfigMap`, `reconcile_fernet.go:122`). Model B's generator (e.g. `scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh`) is *simpler* than the key-rotation scripts — it generates a strong random password (stdlib only, CC-0013, e.g. `python -c 'import secrets…'`) and PATCHes it onto the staging Secret with the `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` annotation. There is **no** `keystone-manage` invocation and **no** database migration step (contrast `credential_rotate.sh`, whose `credential_migrate` step is load-bearing) — the password is opaque to Keystone until `keystone-manage bootstrap` consumes it via Part 1.

8. **The single shared `bootstrap/keystone-admin` path is a known multi-CR / parallel-e2e hazard.** Unlike the per-CR Fernet/credential paths (`openstack/keystone/{name}/…`, made per-CR by CC-0093 specifically to stop two CRs sharing one backing object), the admin password ExternalSecret is hardcoded to the single flat path `bootstrap/keystone-admin` in a fixed `openstack` namespace. Two Keystone CRs with Model B enabled would both push to the same OpenBao object and clobber each other, and parallel e2e suites would race on it (this matches the existing shared-OpenBao-KV-path flake class). This issue must make an explicit design decision: either (a) accept the single-admin / single-CR assumption and document it as a precondition for enabling Model B, or (b) make the push path per-CR (mirroring CC-0093) and reconcile that with the hardcoded ExternalSecret. Option (a) matches today's reality (one cluster admin, one hardcoded ExternalSecret) and is the recommended scope; option (b) is a larger change and likely out of scope here.

**Motivation:**

Part 1 shrinks the blast radius of an admin-credential compromise from "until cluster rebuild" to "until next ESO refresh + reconcile + bootstrap-Job lifetime" — but only *if someone rotates the password*. A rotation mechanism that still depends on a human running `bao kv put` on a remembered cadence is a process control, not a technical control: it degrades silently the moment the runbook is forgotten, and it leaves the single most privileged cloud credential static for as long as nobody acts. Model B converts that process control into an enforced schedule. With `spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.enabled: true`, the admin password rotates automatically on the configured cadence, the rotation is observable through the same condition/metrics surface as Fernet and credential rotation, and "the admin password has not been rotated in N months" stops being a recurring audit finding (OWASP A07 — long-lived authentication credential).

Crucially this reuses machinery that already exists and is battle-tested in production: the split-compute-write CronJob, the operator-owned staging Secret, the least-privilege RBAC split, and the `PushSecret`-to-OpenBao path are all shipped for Fernet/credential keys under CC-0081/CC-0083/CC-0093. The marginal cost of Model B is one new (simpler) generation script, one feature-gated sub-reconciler that wires those primitives together, one CRD field, and one narrowly-scoped OpenBao write policy.

**Affected Areas:**

- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` — add `PasswordRotationSpec` and the `PasswordRotation *PasswordRotationSpec` field on `BootstrapSpec` (`:388-407`); mirror the leaf-default convention of `FernetSpec`/`CredentialKeysSpec`/`TrustFlushSpec`.
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go`, `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/`, `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/` — regenerate from the new spec field; keep the three CRD representations in lockstep (`check-crd-drift`).
- `operators/keystone/internal/webhook/` (defaulting + validating webhooks) — default `schedule` when `passwordRotation.enabled` and `schedule` empty; reject malformed cron; enforce Part-1 prerequisite invariants.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_passwordrotation.go` (new) — feature-gated sub-reconciler mirroring `reconcile_fernet.go`: ensure push-source Secret, staging Secret, split RBAC, generation CronJob, and the `bootstrap/keystone-admin` `PushSecret`; set `PasswordRotationReady`; tear everything down when disabled.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh` (new) — stdlib-only strong-password generator that PATCHes the staging Secret with the `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` annotation (CC-0073, CC-0013); add to the script lint/test harness.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go` — register the new sub-reconciler in the step chain and add `PasswordRotationReady` to `subConditionTypes`.
- `operators/keystone/internal/metrics/` — add `PasswordRotationReady` to the `subReconcilerConditionTypes` label map (`check-condition-coverage`); optionally extend the `keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds` gauge with a `keyType="admin-password"` series fed from the push-source Secret's `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` annotation.
- `deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl` (new) — narrowly-scoped `create`/`update`/`read`/`delete` on `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin` and `kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin`; bound alongside the admin-password `PushSecret`'s role. `eso-management.hcl` stays read-only.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_passwordrotation_test.go` (new) — unit coverage: resources built when enabled, absent/torn-down when disabled, `suspend` pauses the CronJob, `PasswordRotationReady` transitions, generated CronJob shape (split RBAC, staging-Secret env, PushSecret remote key).
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go` — envtest coverage of enable → CronJob created → (simulated) staging PATCH → apply → push-source Secret updated.
- `tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-scheduled-rotation/` (new Chainsaw suite; the `tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/` directory — `00-keystone-cr.yaml` + `chainsaw-test.yaml` — is the structural template) — enable rotation, drive/trigger a CronJob run, observe the new password reach OpenBao and round-trip through ESO into the `keystone-admin` Secret, observe Part 1 re-bootstrap, assert the new password authenticates (HTTP 201) and the old one fails (HTTP 401). Must account for boundary 8's single-shared-path hazard in test isolation.
- `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md` — document the `reconcilePasswordRotation` step, the `passwordRotation` spec, the CronJob/staging/RBAC/PushSecret topology, and the `PasswordRotationReady` condition.
- `docs/guides/keystone-key-rotation.md` (or a sibling guide) — operator-facing how-to for enabling scheduled admin-password rotation and the observable evidence (CronJob runs, OpenBao value change, ESO sync, re-bootstrap, post-rotation token check).
- `architecture/docs/09-implementation/05-keystone-dependencies.md` and `architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md` (in the `architecture/` submodule) — mark Model B as implemented under "Admin Credential Rotation" / Credential-Rotation mechanism 3; bump the submodule pointer.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] `spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation` exists with `enabled` (default `false`), `schedule` (cron), and `suspend` (default `false`). The CRD schema change is regenerated across all three representations (`zz_generated.deepcopy.go`, `config/crd/bases/`, Helm `crds/`) with no drift.
- [ ] With `passwordRotation.enabled: false` (the default), **no** Model B resources exist and behaviour is byte-for-byte the Part-1 (#381) steady state. Toggling from `true` back to `false` tears down the CronJob, staging Secret, push-source Secret, RBAC, and `PushSecret`.
- [ ] With `passwordRotation.enabled: true`, the operator creates: a CronJob on the configured `schedule`, an operator-owned staging Secret (CronJob owns only `.data` via `get`+`patch`; no `create`/`delete`), a read-only RBAC grant on the push-source Secret, and a `PushSecret` targeting `bootstrap/keystone-admin`. The split-RBAC shape matches `ensureFernetRotationRBAC` (`reconcile_fernet.go:159-217`).
- [ ] The defaulting webhook materializes a default `schedule` when `enabled` is true and `schedule` is empty; the validating webhook rejects a malformed cron expression.
- [ ] `suspend: true` pauses the CronJob (`spec.suspend`) without deleting it or any sibling resource, matching `TrustFlushSpec.Suspend` semantics.
- [ ] End-to-end: enabling rotation and triggering a CronJob run results in a new password written to `kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin`, synced by the existing `keystone-admin` ExternalSecret into the `keystone-admin` Secret, and applied by Part 1's re-bootstrap such that the `admin` user authenticates against `POST /v3/auth/tokens` with the new password (HTTP 201) and the old password fails (HTTP 401).
- [ ] The push-source Secret is a **distinct** object from the ESO-Owned `keystone-admin` Secret, so the `PushSecret` writer and ESO's `creationPolicy: Owner` reconciler never fight over the same object.
- [ ] A `PasswordRotationReady` status condition is set by the sub-reconciler, included in the aggregate `subConditionTypes`, and present in the `subReconcilerConditionTypes` metrics-label map (Prometheus `condition_type` resolves, never `UNKNOWN`). Verified by the `check-condition-coverage` gate.
- [ ] A new OpenBao policy grants `create`/`update`/`read`/`delete` on both `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin` and `kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin`, scoped to that exact path. `eso-management.hcl` remains read-only; the CC-0083 audit invariant is preserved and the higher-trust grant is documented.
- [ ] The generation script is extracted under `scripts/`, embedded via `//go:embed`, materialized through an immutable ConfigMap, uses stdlib only (CC-0013/CC-0073), performs **no** `keystone-manage` call and **no** DB migration, and stamps the `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` commit annotation atomically with its staging PATCH.
- [ ] Unit tests cover enabled/disabled/suspend, the generated CronJob/staging/RBAC/PushSecret shape, and `PasswordRotationReady` transitions. Envtest covers the enable → CronJob → staging-PATCH → apply → push-source-Secret-updated chain.
- [ ] A Chainsaw E2E suite under `tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-scheduled-rotation/` exercises the full scheduled-rotation path and respects the single-shared-`bootstrap/keystone-admin`-path isolation constraint (boundary 8).
- [ ] The multi-CR / single-shared-path decision (boundary 8) is made explicitly and documented: either Model B is documented as requiring a single Keystone CR per `bootstrap/keystone-admin` path, or the push path is made per-CR.
- [ ] Reconciler reference and operator guide document the feature; the `architecture/` submodule chapters are updated and the submodule pointer is bumped.

**Non-Goals:**

- Part 1 itself (#381). Model B depends on it but does not re-implement the re-bootstrap-on-Secret-change path, the password-content hash, or the `PodSpecHash` change.
- Rotating service users (nova, neutron, cinder, …) or application credentials. Those use their own service users / application credentials, not the admin password, and require a different mechanism.
- Revoking already-issued Fernet tokens on rotation. A password change does not invalidate existing tokens; immediate session invalidation is a separate operation (`openstack token revoke`, or a Fernet primary-key rotation via the existing CronJob).
- Changing ESO's `refreshInterval` from 1h. Operators who need a faster cutover after a scheduled rotation can force-sync the ExternalSecret (`kubectl annotate externalsecret keystone-admin force-sync=…`); an SLA-driven interval change is a deployment-wide decision out of scope here.
- Migrating the `keystone-admin` Secret away from `creationPolicy: Owner`. The correct invariant remains "write to OpenBao first, let ESO own the in-cluster Secret"; Model B writes to OpenBao via a `PushSecret` and does not relax `Owner`.
- Adding a controller-side OpenBao client. Delivery into OpenBao is done declaratively via a `PushSecret` (the established CC-0083 pattern), not via direct operator→OpenBao API calls.
- Making the `bootstrap/keystone-admin` OpenBao path per-CR as a hard requirement. The recommended scope (boundary 8, option a) is the single-admin / single-CR assumption; a per-CR path layout is a larger follow-up if multi-CR admin rotation is ever needed.
- Changing the password generation primitive beyond "strong random from the language stdlib". No HSM, no external password vault, no complexity-policy engine.

**References:**

- Source issue / Part 1 — re-bootstrap on admin Secret change (#381)
- Fernet split-compute-write sub-reconciler — primary template (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:45-461`)
- Credential split-compute-write sibling (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go:52-465`)
- Staging-Secret + apply primitives: `ensureStagingSecret`, `applyRotationOutput`, `StagingSecretLabelKey`, `RotationCompletedAnnotation` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/rotation_staging.go:29-269`)
- Split-RBAC pattern (read-only production, get+patch staging) — `ensureFernetRotationRBAC` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:159-217`)
- `PushSecret` to OpenBao via `secrets.EnsurePushSecret` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:135-139`, `fernetKeysPushSecret` `:435-461`)
- `BootstrapSpec` — target for the new `passwordRotation` field (`operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:388-407`)
- Leaf-default + defaulting-webhook convention — `FernetSpec`/`CredentialKeysSpec`/`TrustFlushSpec` (`operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:332-380`)
- `keystone-admin` ExternalSecret (1h refresh, `creationPolicy: Owner`, path `bootstrap/keystone-admin`) (`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml`)
- OpenBao read-only bootstrap grant — must stay read-only (`deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl`)
- OpenBao write-policy template + KV-v2 data/metadata rationale (`deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl`)
- Extracted-script + immutable-ConfigMap convention CC-0073 (`reconcile_fernet.go:40,122`, `scripts/fernet_rotate.sh`)
- Bootstrap reconciler (Part 1 consumer of the new password) (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go`)
- Chainsaw rotation template (`tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml`, `00-keystone-cr.yaml`)
- Relevant feature IDs: CC-0081 (split-compute-write), CC-0083 (PushSecret write policy + audit invariant), CC-0093 (per-CR OpenBao paths), CC-0073 (extracted scripts), CC-0013 (stdlib-only). A new CC-NNNN is assigned at implementation time.
- OWASP A07 (Identification and Authentication Failures) — scheduled rotation of the longest-lived privileged credential


**Labels:** enhancement

stories.title: Security operator enables scheduled admin-password rotation
stories.want: to opt a Keystone CR into automatic, scheduled rotation of the admin password via spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.enabled=true
stories.so_that: the most privileged cloud credential rotates on an enforced cadence instead of depending on a human remembering to run `bao kv put` (OWASP A07)
criteria: Setting spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.enabled=true creates a CronJob named <keystone>-admin-password-rotate on the configured (or defaulted) schedule
An operator-owned staging Secret <keystone>-admin-password-rotation is created with the forge.c5c3.io/rotation-target=admin-password label and no .data
A split RBAC Role grants the CronJob ServiceAccount get-only on the push-source Secret and get+patch on the staging Secret only (no create/delete, no production-Secret write)
A PushSecret targeting OpenBao remoteKey bootstrap/keystone-admin is created once a valid password exists in the push-source Secret
spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation is absent by default and enabling it is the only way these resources appear

stories.title: Operator disables or suspends rotation and observes clean teardown
stories.want: to turn rotation off (enabled=false) or pause it (suspend=true) and have the operator return to a predictable steady state
stories.so_that: I can safely back out of the feature or pause it during incidents without orphaned CronJobs or RBAC
criteria: With enabled=false (default), NO Model B resources exist and behaviour is byte-for-byte the Part-1 (CC-0108) steady state
Toggling enabled true->false deletes the CronJob, staging Secret, push-source Secret, ServiceAccount/Role/RoleBinding, PushSecret, and script ConfigMap
suspend=true sets CronJob.spec.suspend=true and deletes nothing — the staging Secret, push-source Secret, RBAC and PushSecret remain
PasswordRotationReady is set to True with reason RotationDisabled when disabled, so the aggregate Ready condition is not held False

stories.title: SRE observes rotation health through condition and metrics surface
stories.want: the password-rotation sub-reconciler to expose a PasswordRotationReady condition and resolve its Prometheus condition_type label
stories.so_that: rotation is observable through the same surface as Fernet and credential rotation and 'condition_type=UNKNOWN' never appears
criteria: PasswordRotationReady appears in keystone_controller.go subConditionTypes and contributes to the aggregate Ready
subReconcilerConditionTypes maps "PasswordRotation" -> "PasswordRotationReady" so the metrics label resolves
The check-condition-coverage gate and the TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames/CoversAllCallSites AST drift-guards pass
When a rotation is applied, keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds optionally publishes a key_type="admin-password" series fed from the push-source Secret's rotation-completed annotation

stories.title: Security auditor verifies least-privilege OpenBao write grant
stories.want: the new OpenBao write capability for the admin bootstrap path to be narrowly scoped and to leave the read-only management invariant intact
stories.so_that: the higher-trust admin-bootstrap write grant is isolated and a leaked eso-management token alone still cannot write to OpenBao (CC-0083)
criteria: A new policy deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl grants create/update/read/delete on exactly kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin AND kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin
deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl remains read-only on kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*
The new policy is bound only by appending ,push-keystone-admin to the management cluster token_policies in deploy/openbao/bootstrap/setup-auth.sh, alongside push-keystone-keys
The push-source Secret is a distinct object from the ESO creationPolicy:Owner keystone-admin Secret so the PushSecret writer and ESO never fight over one object

stories.title: Maintainer keeps the CRD schema and webhooks consistent
stories.want: the new spec field regenerated across all three CRD representations and learned by the defaulting and validating webhooks
stories.so_that: the cluster never rejects a CRD that is older than the controller and malformed input is caught at admission
criteria: zz_generated.deepcopy.go, config/crd/bases/, and helm/keystone-operator/crds/ are regenerated and the check-crd-drift gate reports no drift
The defaulting webhook materializes the default schedule when passwordRotation.enabled is true and schedule is empty
The validating webhook rejects a malformed cron expression via cron.ParseStandard with a field-specific error
Unit tests cover the new deepcopy, defaulting, and validation paths

stories.title: Operator verifies an end-to-end scheduled rotation authenticates
stories.want: a Chainsaw suite that drives a CronJob run and proves the new password round-trips OpenBao->ESO->Secret->re-bootstrap and authenticates
stories.so_that: the full Model B path is proven working and the old password is conclusively retired
criteria: Triggering the CronJob (kubectl create job --from=cronjob) produces a new value at kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin (the suite's dedicated path)
The dedicated ExternalSecret syncs it into the admin Secret and Part-1 re-bootstrap runs (BootstrapReady cuts to True)
POST /v3/auth/tokens returns 201 with the new password and 401 with the old password
The suite respects the single-shared-bootstrap-path isolation constraint (boundary 8) and is not run concurrently with other writers of that path

stories.so_that: I can enable and operate scheduled rotation without reading the controller source
criteria: docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md has a ### reconcilePasswordRotation section mirroring the reconcileFernetKeys template (Steps, tables, Condition Contract, RBAC split)

An operator how-to documents enabling spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation and the evidence chain (CronJob run, OpenBao value change, ESO sync, re-bootstrap, 201/401 check), cross-linking the existing manual-rotation guide rather than duplicating it
The single-CR precondition (boundary 8) is documented
The architecture/ submodule chapters are updated in the C5C3/C5C3 repo and the forge submodule pointer is bumped (submodule is read-only from forge)

requirements.description: The Keystone CRD SHALL expose spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation (PasswordRotationSpec) with enabled (default false), schedule (cron, default "0 0 1 * *"), suspend (default false), and an optional passwordLength (default 32, minimum 24); the schema change SHALL be regenerated across zz_generated.deepcopy.go, config/crd/bases/, and helm/keystone-operator/crds/ with no drift.
requirements.rationale: A feature-gated leaf-default field mirroring TrustFlushSpec; a real CRD schema change must stay in lockstep across all three representations or the cluster rejects the CRD (check-crd-drift hard gate).

scenarios.name: Field present and defaulted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.enabled=true and no schedule
scenarios.then: the stored object carries schedule="0 0 1 * *", suspend=false, passwordLength=32
and_then: the +kubebuilder:default markers fire as defense-in-depth when the webhook is bypassed (envtest)

scenarios.name: Absent by default
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR omits spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation entirely
scenarios.then: the pointer is nil and no defaulting materializes it (feature is off)
and_then: no Model B resources are created

scenarios.name: CRD representations in lockstep
scenarios.when: make generate manifests is run after adding the field
scenarios.then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go gains PasswordRotationSpec DeepCopy funcs and BootstrapSpec.DeepCopyInto gains a nil-checked pointer block, and both CRD YAML copies gain the passwordRotation schema
and_then: the check-crd-drift skill reports no drift across the three representations

requirements.description: With passwordRotation disabled (enabled=false or the block absent) the sub-reconciler SHALL be a clean no-op that tears down any previously-created CronJob, staging Secret, push-source Secret, RBAC (ServiceAccount/Role/RoleBinding), PushSecret, and script ConfigMap, and SHALL set PasswordRotationReady=True with reason RotationDisabled.
requirements.rationale: Disabling must return to the Part-1-only steady state and must not hold the aggregate Ready False (subConditionTypes requires all members True).

scenarios.name: Disabled is a no-op
scenarios.when: passwordRotation is absent and reconcilePasswordRotation runs
scenarios.then: no CronJob/staging/push-source/RBAC/PushSecret/ConfigMap is created and PasswordRotationReady=True reason RotationDisabled
and_then: the aggregate Ready condition can still reach True

scenarios.name: Toggle true->false tears down
scenarios.when: a CR previously had enabled=true and is updated to enabled=false
scenarios.then: the CronJob, staging Secret, push-source Secret, ServiceAccount, Role, RoleBinding, PushSecret, and script ConfigMap are deleted (NotFound tolerated)
and_then: a subsequent reconcile observes none of those objects

scenarios.name: Teardown is idempotent
scenarios.when: reconcilePasswordRotation runs twice on a disabled CR with nothing left to delete
scenarios.then: each delete tolerates NotFound and returns no error
and_then: PasswordRotationReady stays True reason RotationDisabled

requirements.description: With passwordRotation.enabled=true the operator SHALL ensure: an operator-owned staging Secret, an operator-owned push-source Secret distinct from keystone-admin, a split RBAC Role (get on the push-source Secret, get+patch on the staging Secret only), a generation CronJob on the configured schedule, an immutable ConfigMap carrying the embedded generation script, and (clobber-safe) a PushSecret to bootstrap/keystone-admin; and SHALL set PasswordRotationReady=True.
requirements.rationale: Reuses the five Fernet/credential split-compute-write primitives (ensureStagingSecret, split RBAC, CronJob, applyRotationOutput-analog, PushSecret) with the production sink being OpenBao instead of an in-cluster Secret.

scenarios.name: Resources built when enabled
scenarios.when: enabled=true and reconcilePasswordRotation runs to completion
scenarios.then: the staging Secret, push-source Secret, SA/Role/RoleBinding, CronJob, and script ConfigMap exist with the operator as owner
and_then: PasswordRotationReady=True reason AdminPasswordRotationConfigured

scenarios.name: Split RBAC shape
scenarios.when: the rotation Role is inspected
scenarios.then: rule 1 = verbs [get] on the push-source Secret name; rule 2 = verbs [get,patch] on the staging Secret name; no create/delete and no production-Secret write
and_then: the shape matches ensureFernetRotationRBAC (reconcile_fernet.go:159-217)

scenarios.name: CronJob env points at staging
scenarios.when: the generated CronJob is inspected
scenarios.then: SECRET_NAME=<keystone>-admin-password-rotation, SECRET_NAMESPACE via fieldRef metadata.namespace, Command=[/scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh], serviceAccountName=<keystone>-admin-password-rotate
and_then: the CronJob SA can patch only the staging Secret, never the push-source or keystone-admin Secret

requirements.description: The defaulting webhook SHALL materialize the default schedule when passwordRotation.enabled is true and schedule is empty (and default passwordLength when zero), and the validating webhook SHALL reject a malformed cron expression in schedule via cron.ParseStandard with a field-specific error; the webhook SHALL NOT materialize the passwordRotation block when it is absent (the feature stays off).
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the TrustFlush/Fernet/Credential cron-default + cron-validate precedent in keystone_webhook.go, but non-mutating when absent because the feature defaults disabled.

scenarios.name: Default schedule on enable
scenarios.when: a CR is admitted with passwordRotation.enabled=true and schedule empty
scenarios.then: Default() sets schedule=DefaultPasswordRotationSchedule ("0 0 1 * *")
and_then: passwordLength is set to 32 if zero

scenarios.name: Malformed cron rejected
scenarios.when: a CR is admitted with passwordRotation.enabled=true and schedule="not a cron"
scenarios.then: ValidateCreate/Update returns an Invalid error on spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.schedule
and_then: the error message includes the cron parse failure

scenarios.name: Absent block not materialized
scenarios.when: a CR is admitted without spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation
scenarios.then: Default() leaves the pointer nil and validate() skips the cron check
and_then: the CR is admitted unchanged

requirements.description: When suspend=true the generated CronJob SHALL set spec.suspend=true and the operator SHALL delete no sibling resource, matching TrustFlushSpec.Suspend semantics; PasswordRotationReady SHALL remain True.
requirements.rationale: Pause-without-delete is the documented operator affordance; precedent is trustFlushCronJob setting Spec.Suspend=ptr.To(...).

scenarios.name: Suspend pauses
scenarios.when: enabled=true and suspend=true
scenarios.then: the CronJob exists with spec.suspend=true and the staging/push-source/RBAC/PushSecret/ConfigMap are unchanged
and_then: PasswordRotationReady=True

scenarios.name: Unsuspend resumes
scenarios.when: suspend is flipped true->false
scenarios.then: the CronJob spec.suspend becomes false on the next reconcile
and_then: no resource is recreated, only the field updated

scenarios.name: Suspend differs from disable
scenarios.when: suspend=true (still enabled)
scenarios.then: nothing is deleted (contrast REQ-002 teardown)
and_then: the difference is asserted by separate unit tests

requirements.description: The generation script SHALL live at scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh, be embedded via //go:embed, materialized through config.CreateImmutableConfigMap, use only the language standard library (CC-0013) to generate a strong random password, PATCH it onto the staging Secret's data.password, stamp the forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at annotation atomically in the same strategic-merge PATCH, and perform NO keystone-manage invocation and NO database migration step.
requirements.rationale: Follows the extracted-script convention (CC-0073); the credential_migrate step that is load-bearing for credential keys MUST NOT be copied because the admin password is opaque to Keystone until Part-1 re-bootstrap consumes it.

scenarios.name: Stdlib-only generation and atomic PATCH
scenarios.when: the embedded script content is asserted
scenarios.then: it begins with a POSIX shebang and SPDX header, set -eu, uses python3 secrets (e.g. token_urlsafe) for the password, and a single strategic-merge PATCH writes data.password and the rotation-completed annotation together
and_then: it contains no `keystone-manage` and no `credential_migrate`/db_sync call

scenarios.name: Embedded and lintable
scenarios.when: the package is built and `make shellcheck` runs
scenarios.then: adminPasswordRotateScript is non-empty (go:embed) and the script passes shellcheck --severity=warning via the existing operators/*/internal/controller/scripts/*.sh glob
and_then: no Makefile or CI edit is required to lint or discover it

scenarios.name: Materialized via immutable ConfigMap
scenarios.when: the CronJob mounts the script
scenarios.then: config.CreateImmutableConfigMap produces a versioned ConfigMap whose data key is admin_password_rotate.sh mounted at /scripts (DefaultMode 0o555)
and_then: the CronJob Command is /scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh

requirements.description: The operator-side apply step SHALL validate the staging Secret (rotation-completed annotation present and parseable; a non-empty password of at least the minimum length) and SHALL commit the new password onto the dedicated push-source Secret (e.g. <keystone>-admin-password-next), then delete the staging Secret; the PushSecret SHALL sync the push-source Secret to OpenBao remoteKey bootstrap/keystone-admin; the push-source Secret SHALL be a distinct object from the ESO creationPolicy:Owner keystone-admin Secret.
requirements.rationale: validateRotationOutput is fernet-key-shaped (44-byte base64url, key-count range) and not reusable for a password, so an additive password-shaped validator and apply variant are required; the push-source Secret must differ from keystone-admin or the PushSecret writer and ESO's Owner reconciler fight every refresh.

scenarios.name: Valid staging applied to push-source
scenarios.when: the staging Secret has data.password (>= minimum length) and a parseable rotation-completed annotation
scenarios.then: the push-source Secret's data.password is replaced, the rotation-completed annotation is stamped on it, the staging Secret is deleted, and an AdminPasswordRotated event is emitted
and_then: the next reconcile short-circuits with requeue (mirrors applyRotationOutput)

scenarios.name: Invalid staging rejected, not applied
scenarios.when: the staging Secret password is empty or below minimum length, or the annotation is malformed
scenarios.then: a Warning event is emitted, the push-source Secret is unchanged, and the staging Secret is retained for inspection
and_then: no apply occurs and reconcile does not error

scenarios.name: Push-source distinct from keystone-admin
scenarios.when: the push-source Secret and the PushSecret selector are inspected
scenarios.then: the push-source Secret name differs from spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.Name (keystone-admin)
and_then: the PushSecret RemoteKey is bootstrap/keystone-admin

requirements.description: A new OpenBao policy deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl SHALL grant create/update/read/delete on both kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin and kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin and SHALL be bound only by appending ,push-keystone-admin to the management cluster token_policies in deploy/openbao/bootstrap/setup-auth.sh; deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl SHALL remain read-only on kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*.
requirements.rationale: ESO's KV-v2 provider writes both the data and metadata paths and DELETEs both on cleanup; the separate narrowly-scoped write policy preserves the CC-0083 audit invariant that an eso-management token alone cannot write to OpenBao.

scenarios.name: Narrow data+metadata write grant
scenarios.when: push-keystone-admin.hcl is reviewed
scenarios.then: exactly two path stanzas grant create/update/read/delete on kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin and kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin
and_then: no wildcard over bootstrap/* and no other bootstrap leaf is writable

scenarios.name: Bound alongside on management role only
scenarios.when: setup-auth.sh is reviewed
scenarios.then: the management-cluster branch appends ,push-keystone-admin to token_policies next to ,push-keystone-keys; non-management clusters keep only read-only eso-<cluster>
and_then: setup-policies.sh auto-loads the new .hcl by basename with no edit

scenarios.name: Read-only invariant preserved
scenarios.when: eso-management.hcl is reviewed
scenarios.then: kv-v2/data/bootstrap/* still grants only read
and_then: the CC-0083 invariant is intact

requirements.description: The sub-reconciler SHALL set a PasswordRotationReady status condition that is included in keystone_controller.go subConditionTypes and mapped in the instrumentation.go subReconcilerConditionTypes label map so the Prometheus condition_type label resolves and never shows UNKNOWN.
requirements.rationale: Hard gate enforced by check-condition-coverage and by the TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames / CoversAllCallSites AST drift-guards.

scenarios.name: Condition aggregated
scenarios.when: PasswordRotationReady is added to subConditionTypes
scenarios.then: the aggregate Ready (AllTrue) accounts for it
and_then: disabled rotation still allows Ready=True via reason RotationDisabled

scenarios.name: Metrics label resolves
scenarios.when: a PasswordRotation sub-reconciler error is recorded
scenarios.then: subReconcilerConditionTypes["PasswordRotation"]=="PasswordRotationReady" so condition_type resolves
and_then: the AST drift-guard tests pass

scenarios.name: Step registered and instrumented
scenarios.when: reconcilePasswordRotation is wired via instrumentSubReconciler("PasswordRotation", ...)
scenarios.then: the call-site coverage drift-guard finds the matching map key
and_then: duration and error metrics are emitted on every path

requirements.description: A Chainsaw E2E suite under tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-scheduled-rotation/ SHALL enable rotation, trigger a CronJob run, observe the new password reach OpenBao and round-trip via ESO into the admin Secret, observe Part-1 re-bootstrap, and assert the new password authenticates (HTTP 201) and the old password fails (HTTP 401); the suite SHALL respect the single-shared bootstrap path isolation constraint (boundary 8).
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the fernet-rotation structural template and the Part-1 admin-password-rotation auth-assertion pattern; the shared bootstrap/keystone-admin path is a multi-CR/parallel-e2e hazard that must be isolated or serialized.

scenarios.name: Full scheduled round-trip
scenarios.when: the CronJob is force-triggered via kubectl create job --from=cronjob and completes
scenarios.then: the push-source Secret updates, the PushSecret writes the new password to OpenBao, ESO syncs it into the admin Secret, and reconcileBootstrap re-runs (BootstrapReady=True)
and_then: POST /v3/auth/tokens returns 201 with the new password and 401 with the old one

scenarios.name: Isolation respected
scenarios.when: the suite runs
scenarios.then: it does not collide with other writers of bootstrap/keystone-admin (serialized / sole-writer, dedicated CR, cache disabled, short ExternalSecret refreshInterval)
and_then: the suite is stable under the e2e runner's parallelism

scenarios.name: Push-source distinctness asserted
scenarios.when: the suite inspects the Secrets
scenarios.then: the push-source Secret is a different object from the admin Secret
and_then: no Owner-vs-PushSecret fight is observed

requirements.description: The operator SHALL never deliver an empty or invalid password to OpenBao: the PushSecret to bootstrap/keystone-admin SHALL only be ensured once the push-source Secret holds a non-empty, valid password, and the apply step SHALL refuse to commit an empty/short password to the push-source Secret.
requirements.rationale: Shadow-path failure mode: an empty value reaching bootstrap/keystone-admin would round-trip into the keystone-admin Secret and re-bootstrap to an empty/garbage password, locking out the admin account cluster-wide.

scenarios.name: No push before first valid rotation
scenarios.when: rotation is enabled but no CronJob run has produced a valid password yet (push-source Secret has no password)
scenarios.then: the PushSecret to bootstrap/keystone-admin is not created (or carries no data) so the existing OpenBao value is untouched
and_then: PasswordRotationReady=True with a reason indicating awaiting first rotation

scenarios.name: Empty staging never committed
scenarios.when: the staging Secret password is empty
scenarios.then: the apply validator rejects it and the push-source Secret is not updated
and_then: nothing is pushed to OpenBao

scenarios.name: Valid password flows
scenarios.when: a valid password reaches the push-source Secret
scenarios.then: the PushSecret is ensured and syncs it to OpenBao
and_then: the ESO round-trip and re-bootstrap proceed

requirements.description: The operator SHOULD publish a keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds series with key_type="admin-password" fed from the push-source Secret's forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at annotation, reusing observeRotationAge.
requirements.rationale: Parity with the Fernet/credential rotation-age observability so 'admin password not rotated in N months' is alertable via absent()/age.

scenarios.name: Age published after apply
scenarios.when: an admin-password rotation has been applied to the push-source Secret
scenarios.then: observeRotationAge sets the gauge with key_type="admin-password" from the rotation-completed annotation
and_then: the gauge tracks time.Since(completedAt) on each reconcile

scenarios.name: No series before first rotation
scenarios.when: no rotation has completed
scenarios.then: no admin-password gauge series is published (best-effort)
and_then: absent() remains the 'never rotated' alert signal

scenarios.name: Series cleaned up on CR delete
scenarios.when: the Keystone CR is deleted
scenarios.then: DeleteForKeystone drops the admin-password series along with other per-CR metrics
and_then: no stale gauge remains

requirements.description: Documentation SHALL be added: a reconcilePasswordRotation section in docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md, an operator how-to for enabling scheduled rotation (extending the existing manual-rotation guide), the single-CR precondition, and the architecture/ submodule chapters marked implemented with the forge submodule pointer bumped.
requirements.rationale: Diataxis reference + how-to coverage; architecture/ is a read-only submodule so its chapters change in the C5C3/C5C3 repo and forge only bumps the pointer.

scenarios.name: Reference section present
scenarios.when: keystone-reconciler.md is reviewed
scenarios.then: a ### reconcilePasswordRotation section documents File/Signature/Steps/Condition Contract/RBAC split and is referenced from the flow diagram and dependency graph
and_then: it cross-links reconcileBootstrap as the consumer of the new password

scenarios.name: How-to present and non-duplicative
scenarios.when: the operator guide is reviewed
scenarios.then: it documents enabling spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation and the evidence chain, reusing/cross-linking the existing manual-rotation steps rather than copying them
and_then: it cites the new Chainsaw suite as proof

scenarios.name: Architecture marked implemented via pointer bump
scenarios.when: the architecture chapters are updated
scenarios.then: the '(planned)'/'not yet built' wording for Admin Credential Rotation / mechanism 3 is changed in the C5C3/C5C3 repo and forge bumps the submodule pointer (git add architecture)
and_then: no files under /workspace/architecture are edited directly from the forge worktree

requirements.description: The multi-CR / single-shared-path decision (boundary 8) SHALL be made explicitly and documented: Model B adopts option (a) — the push path is the single flat bootstrap/keystone-admin and Model B is documented as requiring at most one Model-B-enabled Keystone CR per cluster.
requirements.rationale: Two Model-B-enabled CRs would both push to the same OpenBao object and clobber each other; option (a) matches today's hardcoded ExternalSecret reality and is the recommended scope.

scenarios.name: Decision documented
scenarios.when: the reference/how-to docs are reviewed
scenarios.then: the single-CR precondition is stated explicitly with the rationale
and_then: the per-CR path alternative (option b) is recorded as deferred

scenarios.name: Hardcoded remote key
scenarios.when: the PushSecret builder is inspected
scenarios.then: RemoteKey is the literal bootstrap/keystone-admin (not per-CR)
and_then: it matches the hardcoded keystone-admin ExternalSecret remoteRef

scenarios.name: E2E reflects the constraint
scenarios.when: the Chainsaw suite runs
scenarios.then: it is the sole writer of its bootstrap path and is serialized accordingly
and_then: no parallel-suite race on the shared path occurs

tasks.title: Add PasswordRotationSpec type and BootstrapSpec.PasswordRotation pointer field (REQ-001) in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go, mirroring TrustFlushSpec leaf-default markers (enabled default false, schedule default "0 0 1 * *", suspend default false, passwordLength default 32 Minimum 24). Add a DeepCopy round-trip unit test in keystone_types_test.go.

Regenerate CRD artifacts (REQ-001): run make generate manifests so zz_generated.deepcopy.go gains PasswordRotationSpec funcs + BootstrapSpec pointer block (KeystoneSpec.DeepCopyInto Bootstrap value-copy -> DeepCopyInto), and config/crd/bases/ + helm/keystone-operator/crds/ gain the schema. Verify with the check-crd-drift skill.

Teach the defaulting webhook (REQ-004) in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go: add DefaultPasswordRotationSchedule const ("0 0 1 * *"); in Default(), when bootstrap.passwordRotation!=nil && enabled && schedule=="" set schedule (and passwordLength=32 if 0); do NOT materialize the block when nil. Unit test in keystone_webhook_test.go.

Teach the validating webhook (REQ-004, REQ-014): in validate(), when passwordRotation!=nil && enabled, require schedule non-empty and reject a malformed cron via cron.ParseStandard with a spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.schedule field error; require adminPasswordSecretRef.Name set. Unit tests for malformed/valid/absent.

Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh (REQ-006): POSIX sh + SPDX + set -eu; generate a strong password with python3 secrets (token_urlsafe, length from env) and PATCH the staging Secret data.password + forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at atomically via the in-cluster K8s API with the SA token (copy the python PATCH block from fernet_rotate.sh, drop keystone-manage). Add //go:embed var adminPasswordRotateScript and TestAdminPasswordRotateScript_EmbeddedContent (mirror reconcile_fernet_test.go:860).

Create reconcile_passwordrotation.go (REQ-003) with name helpers (adminPasswordStagingSecretName, adminPasswordNextSecretName), ensure the operator-owned push-source Secret, ensureStagingSecret(...,"admin-password"), and ensureAdminPasswordRotationRBAC mirroring ensureFernetRotationRBAC (get on push-source, get+patch on staging). Unit tests for resource presence and split-RBAC shape.

Add adminPasswordRotationCronJob builder (REQ-003, REQ-005): schedule from spec, Spec.Suspend=ptr.To(spec.PasswordRotation.Suspend), serviceAccountName=<keystone>-admin-password-rotate, env SECRET_NAME=staging + SECRET_NAMESPACE fieldRef, script ConfigMap mount at /scripts (0o555), restrictedSecurityContext, Command=[/scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh]. Unit test for CronJob shape + suspend.

Add validateAdminPasswordRotationOutput (password present, non-empty, >= minimum length) and applyAdminPasswordRotation (REQ-007, REQ-011): mirror applyRotationOutput steps but validate the password and commit onto the push-source Secret (replace data, stamp annotation), delete staging, emit AdminPasswordRotated; reject empty/short with a Warning and retain staging. Unit tests for valid commit and reject paths. (Additive; does NOT touch applyRotationOutput/validateRotationOutput.)

Add adminPasswordPushSecret builder + clobber-safe gating (REQ-007, REQ-011, REQ-014): build a PushSecret (ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store) selecting the push-source Secret with RemoteKey "bootstrap/keystone-admin" (hardcoded, single-CR), and only ensure it once the push-source Secret holds a valid password. Unit test that no PushSecret exists until a valid password is present and that RemoteKey is the flat path.

Implement the reconcilePasswordRotation entry (REQ-003, REQ-009): enabled-path ordering (push-source Secret -> staging Secret -> observeRotationAge("admin-password") -> applyAdminPasswordRotation w/ requeue-on-apply -> RBAC -> immutable ConfigMap from adminPasswordRotateScript -> CronJob -> clobber-safe PushSecret -> set PasswordRotationReady=True). Unit test the happy-path condition and ordering.

Implement the disabled/teardown branch (REQ-002): when passwordRotation==nil || !enabled, delete CronJob (deleteCronJob), staging Secret, push-source Secret, SA/Role/RoleBinding, PushSecret, and script ConfigMap (tolerate NotFound) and set PasswordRotationReady=True reason RotationDisabled. Unit tests for teardown and idempotency.

Wire the sub-reconciler into the controller (REQ-009): register instrumentSubReconciler(ctx,"PasswordRotation", r.reconcilePasswordRotation(...,configMapName)) after Bootstrap/TrustFlush in keystone_controller.go; add "PasswordRotationReady" to subConditionTypes and "PasswordRotation":"PasswordRotationReady" to subReconcilerConditionTypes (instrumentation.go). Confirm the CoversAllNames/CoversAllCallSites drift-guards pass.

Add the key_type="admin-password" rotation-age series (REQ-012): call observeRotationAge with keyType "admin-password" reading the push-source Secret's rotation-completed annotation, and add a metrics unit test for the series + DeleteForKeystone cleanup.

Add the OpenBao write policy (REQ-008): create deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl granting create/update/read/delete on kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin AND kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin (copy push-keystone-keys.hcl:73-87 stanza + rationale); append ",push-keystone-admin" to the management-cluster token_policies in deploy/openbao/bootstrap/setup-auth.sh:92-95; leave eso-management.hcl read-only. Add a shell unit test under tests/unit/deploy/ asserting scope + binding.

Add envtest coverage (REQ-003, REQ-005): integration_test.go cases that enable rotation and assert the CronJob shape (Command /scripts/admin_password_rotate.sh, SECRET_NAME staging) and that suspend=true sets spec.suspend without deleting siblings.

Add envtest apply + teardown coverage (REQ-007, REQ-002): integration_test.go cases that simulate a staging-Secret PATCH and assert the push-source Secret is updated + staging deleted + PushSecret ensured, and that disabling removes all Model B resources.

Create tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-scheduled-rotation/00-keystone-cr.yaml (REQ-010, REQ-014): a dedicated CR + dedicated ExternalSecret on a dedicated OpenBao path with a short refreshInterval (10s), extraConfig cache disabled, and spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.{enabled:true,schedule}. Copy the CC-0108 fixture DECISION header explaining the dedicated-path isolation (boundary 8).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-scheduled-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml (REQ-010, REQ-007, REQ-011): serialized/sole-writer suite — apply CR; assert Ready=True; assert CronJob exists with the schedule; trigger via kubectl create job --from=cronjob + wait complete; assert push-source Secret updated, OpenBao value changed, ESO synced into the admin Secret, Part-1 re-bootstrap (BootstrapReady=True); assert new pw 201 / old pw 401 via the python3 urllib auth_code helper; assert push-source Secret distinct from admin Secret; include the disabled-default no-resources control assertion. Reuse the CC-0108 catch diagnostics blocks.

Document the reconciler (REQ-013): add a ### reconcilePasswordRotation section to docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md mirroring the reconcileFernetKeys template (File/Signature/Steps/per-resource tables/Condition Contract/RBAC split), add the step to the Reconciliation Flow diagram and a Dependency Graph row, reference the existing rotation-target/rotation-completed/admin-password-hash entries, and cross-link reconcileBootstrap as the consumer.

Write the operator how-to (REQ-013, REQ-014): extend docs/guides/keystone-admin-password-rotation.md (or add a sibling keystone-admin-password-scheduled-rotation.md) covering enabling spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation, the CronJob/staging/push-source/PushSecret topology, the evidence chain (CronJob run -> OpenBao change -> ESO sync -> re-bootstrap -> 201/401), the single-CR precondition, and suspend/disable; cross-link the manual guide's steps 3-7 and the new Chainsaw suite rather than duplicating.

Update architecture docs via submodule pointer bump (REQ-013): in the C5C3/C5C3 repo (separate PR) mark Model B implemented in architecture/docs/09-implementation/05-keystone-dependencies.md (Admin Credential Rotation / Model B) and architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md (Credential-Rotation mechanism 3), aligning the annotation key to forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash; then in forge bump the submodule pointer (git add architecture). review_criteria: BLOCKER: No file under /workspace/architecture is edited from the forge worktree; architecture changes land in the C5C3/C5C3 repo and forge only bumps the submodule pointer (rule 12).

BLOCKER: The PushSecret to bootstrap/keystone-admin is never created/active with an empty or invalid password; the apply validator and the clobber-safe gating prevent any empty value from reaching OpenBao (REQ-011).

BLOCKER: eso-management.hcl remains read-only on kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*; the new write grant is confined to push-keystone-admin.hcl on exactly the data+metadata bootstrap/keystone-admin paths, bound only on the management role (REQ-008, CC-0083).

CRITICAL: The push-source Secret is a distinct object from the ESO creationPolicy:Owner keystone-admin Secret, and the CronJob SA has get+patch on the staging Secret only (no production/push-source write, no create/delete) (REQ-003, REQ-007).

CRITICAL: PasswordRotationReady is in subConditionTypes and subReconcilerConditionTypes; check-condition-coverage and the AST drift-guard tests pass; disabled rotation still allows Ready=True (REQ-002, REQ-009).
CRITICAL: CRD schema is regenerated across zz_generated.deepcopy.go, config/crd/bases/, and helm/keystone-operator/crds/ with check-crd-drift reporting no drift (REQ-001).
CRITICAL: applyRotationOutput and validateRotationOutput (fernet/credential) are not modified; Model B uses additive password-shaped variants so existing rotation behaviour is unchanged (REQ-007).

CRITICAL: The generation script is stdlib-only, embedded, ConfigMap-materialized, performs no keystone-manage and no DB migration, and stamps the rotation-completed annotation atomically with the staging PATCH; it passes make shellcheck (REQ-006).
MAJOR: Webhook defaults the schedule only when enabled and rejects malformed cron; the passwordRotation block is not materialized when absent (REQ-004).
MAJOR: The E2E suite respects boundary-8 isolation (dedicated path/CR, cache disabled, short ES refresh, serialized) and asserts 201/401 cutover and push-source distinctness (REQ-010, REQ-014).
MINOR: Every new symbol/test/doc references the Feature ID CC-0109; reused-primitive references cite CC-0081/CC-0083/CC-0093/CC-0073/CC-0013/CC-0108 where relevant.
MINOR: Reference reconciler section and operator how-to exist, document the single-CR precondition, and cross-link rather than duplicate the manual-rotation guide (REQ-013).
implementation_notes:
FEATURE ID: CC-0109 (Part 2 / Model B of #381; Part 1 = CC-0108, already merged on main). This is a near-clone of the Fernet/credential split-compute-write sub-reconcilers; copy the shape, change only the leaf details and the apply sink.

WEBHOOK LOCATION CORRECTION: there is NO operators/keystone/internal/webhook/ directory (the issue's Affected-Areas list is wrong here). The defaulting + validating webhooks live in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go. cron validation uses github.com/robfig/cron/v3 cron.ParseStandard (keystone_webhook.go:326,340,356).

ARCHITECTURE (component relationships):

  Keystone CR (spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation)
     |
     v
  reconcilePasswordRotation (new sub-reconciler, instrumentSubReconciler step)
     |-- ensure push-source Secret  <keystone>-admin-password-next  (operator-owned, distinct from keystone-admin)
     |-- ensure staging Secret      <keystone>-admin-password-rotation (label rotation-target=admin-password, .data owned by CronJob)
     |-- split RBAC: SA/Role/RoleBinding <keystone>-admin-password-rotate (get push-source; get+patch staging)
     |-- immutable ConfigMap: admin_password_rotate.sh
     |-- CronJob <keystone>-admin-password-rotate (schedule, suspend)  --PATCHes-->  staging Secret
     |-- applyAdminPasswordRotation: staging -> push-source (validate, stamp, delete staging)
     |-- PushSecret (clobber-safe)  push-source --> OpenBao kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin
     |-- set PasswordRotationReady

DATA FLOW (one rotation):
  CronJob script (python3 secrets) --PATCH--> staging Secret(.data.password + forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at)
    --operator applyAdminPasswordRotation--> push-source Secret(.data.password) [+annotation]
    --PushSecret/ESO--> OpenBao bootstrap/keystone-admin {password}
    --keystone-admin ExternalSecret (refreshInterval 1h, creationPolicy:Owner)--> keystone-admin Secret(.data.password)
    --Part-1 reconcileBootstrap (sha256(password) -> forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash -> PodSpecHash gate -> job.RunJob re-runs {name}-bootstrap)--> Keystone accepts new password; old password -> 401.

KEY DECISIONS:
- DECISION (apply variant): applyRotationOutput (rotation_staging.go:182-269) calls validateRotationOutput (rotation_validation.go:40) which is fernet-key-shaped (44-byte base64url, 32-byte decode, key-count [min,max], pairwise distinct) and NOT reusable for a password. Model B adds an ADDITIVE applyAdminPasswordRotation + validateAdminPasswordRotationOutput (password present/non-empty/>=min length) rather than refactoring the shared function, to keep the fernet/credential paths untouched (surgical-changes). Sharing via a validator-func parameter is recorded as a deferred refactor.
- DECISION (clobber-safety, REQ-011): the production sink is OpenBao, not an in-cluster Secret, so an empty/invalid push would round-trip into keystone-admin and re-bootstrap to a broken admin credential cluster-wide. The PushSecret is only ensured once the push-source Secret holds a valid password; the apply validator guarantees the push-source never holds an empty/short value. This is the one safety-driven deviation from the always-ensure fernet pattern.
- DECISION (boundary 8): adopt option (a) single flat path. PushSecret RemoteKey is the literal "bootstrap/keystone-admin" matching the hardcoded keystone-admin ExternalSecret; Model B is documented as requiring at most one Model-B-enabled Keystone CR per cluster. Per-CR push paths (option b, mirroring CC-0093) are deferred. Because the path is cluster-global, the E2E suite must be the sole writer of its bootstrap path and run serialized; it uses a dedicated CR + dedicated ExternalSecret + dedicated OpenBao path exactly as the CC-0108 admin-password-rotation suite does.
- DECISION (teardown/condition): disabled is a clean no-op that tears everything down and sets PasswordRotationReady=True reason RotationDisabled so the aggregate Ready (AllTrue over subConditionTypes) is not pinned False. Precedent: reconcileTrustFlush nil-branch + deleteCronJob (reconcile_trustflush.go:36-90,170-178).
- DECISION (step placement): reconcilePasswordRotation has no SYNCHRONOUS dependency on Bootstrap (the new password reaches Bootstrap only via the async OpenBao->ESO round-trip), so it is registered as a standalone instrumentSubReconciler step near the end of the chain (after Bootstrap/TrustFlush), its nearest structural sibling being TrustFlush (feature-gated CronJob with suspend).

PITFALLS:
- The issue says 'a read-only RBAC grant on the push-source Secret' for the CronJob — the CronJob actually needs get+patch on the STAGING Secret; read-only (get) is on the push-source Secret. Follow the fernet shape, not the loose issue wording.
- ESO KV-v2 writes BOTH data and metadata and DELETEs both on cleanup; the policy must grant the metadata path too or the PushSecret 403s on the metadata PUT and never reaches Ready (push-keystone-keys.hcl:58-72 rationale).
- setup-policies.sh auto-loads every policies/*.hcl by basename, so only setup-auth.sh needs a manual edit to bind the new policy.
- The keystone-reconciler.md Bootstrap Job table (lines ~2462-2481) is already partly stale vs code (name keystone-bootstrap vs {name}-bootstrap; RestartPolicy); do not propagate that staleness into the new section.
- E2E without extraConfig.cache.enabled:false can get a false 201/401 from a shared-memcached cached admin lookup under parallelism (CC-0074).
